title: jasminum angulare
intro: Jasminum angulare, also known as the star jasmine or night-blooming jasmine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae, native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its fragrant, star-shaped flowers that bloom in the evening, emitting a strong, sweet scent that attracts pollinators. The medicinal properties of Jasminum angulare include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve sleep quality, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures.
